data.title: Neumora Therapeutics appoints Paul Berns as CEO
data.description:
• Neumora Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NMRA) said its co-founder and executive chair, Paul Berns, will transition to CEO and chair of the board.
  • The firm added Henry Gosebruch will be leaving the company.
  • NMRA said Michael Milligan will serve as chief financial officer.
  • The company said Joshua Pinto will serve as president, and Bill Aurora will serve as chief operating and development officer.

data.title: Brookfield DEPS of $1.01, revenue of $19.43B
data.description:
• Brookfield press release (NYSE:BN): Q4 DEPS of $1.01.
  • Revenue of $19.43B (-20.8% Y/Y).
  • The Board declared a 13% increase in the quarterly dividend for Brookfield Corporation to $0.09 per share (representing $0.36 per annum), payable on March 31, 2025 to shareholders of record as at the close of business on March 14, 2025.

data.title: Thomson Reuters to transfer listing to Nasdaq
data.description:
Thomson Reuters (NYSE:TRI) will voluntarily transfer its U.S. stock exchange listing to the Nasdaq Global Select Market from the New York Stock Exchange.

The company’s common shares will likely cease trading on the NYSE at market close on or about February 24, 2025. Its common shares will commence trading on the Nasdaq on or about February 25, 2025.

Thomson Reuters joined the New York Stock Exchange on April 17, 2008, under the ticker symbol "TRI" after the merger of Thomson Corporation and Reuters Group.

The listing on the Toronto Stock Exchange will not be impacted.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Pagaya Technologies Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.15, revenue of $279.39M beats by $14.25M
data.description:
• Pagaya Technologies press release (NASDAQ:PGY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $279.39M (+28.1% Y/Y) beats by $14.25M.
  • Record network volume of $2.6 billion in 4Q’24 (at the high-end of outlook of ~$2.4 billion to $2.6 billion), grew by 9% year-over-year, driven primarily by Personal Loans.

  • Full Year 2025 Outlook

    FY’25

    Network Volume

    Expected to be between $10.25 billion and $11.75 billion

    Total Revenue and Other Income

    Expected to be between $1.15 billion and $1.275 billion

    Adjusted EBITDA

    Expected to be between $265 million and $315 million

    GAAP Net Income

    Profitability expected in 2Q’25; between ($10) million and $40 million for FY’25

  • First Quarter 2025 Outlook

    1Q’25

    Network Volume

    Expected to be between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion

    Total Revenue and Other Income

    Expected to be between $280 million and $295 million

    Adjusted EBITDA

    Expected to be between $65 million and $75 million

    GAAP Net Income

    Expected to be between ($20) million and breakeven

data.title: Crocs Non-GAAP EPS of $2.52 beats by $0.26, revenue of $990M beats by $28.9M
data.description:
• Crocs press release (NASDAQ:CROX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.52 beats by $0.26.
  • Revenue of $990M (+3.1% Y/Y) beats by $28.9M.
  • Q1 outlook:
  • Revenues to be down approximately 3.5% compared to the first quarter of 2024, at currency rates as of February 10, 2025. This includes an anticipated negative impact of approximately $19 million from foreign currency.
    • Crocs Brand to be down approximately 1% to flat compared to the first quarter of 2024.
    • HEYDUDE Brand to be down approximately 16% to 14% compared to the first quarter of 2024.
  • Adjusted operating margin of approximately 21.5%, including an anticipated negative impact of approximately 80 bps from both foreign currency and announced and pending tariffs.
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share of $2.38 to $2.52.

  • Full Year 2025

    With respect to 2025, we expect:

    • Revenue growth of approximately 2% to 2.5% compared to full year 2024, at currency rates as of February 10, 2025. This includes an anticipated negative impact of approximately $62 million from foreign currency.
      • Crocs Brand to grow approximately 4.5% compared to full year 2024.
      • HEYDUDE Brand to be down approximately 9% to 7% compared to full year 2024.
    • Adjusted operating margin of approximately 24.0%, including an anticipated negative impact of approximately 60 bps from both foreign currency and announced and pending tariffs.
    • Combined GAAP tax rate of approximately 21.5% and non-GAAP effective tax rate of approximately 18.0%.
    • Adjusted diluted earnings per share of $12.70 to $13.15

data.title: European markets extend gains as Trump says Putin agrees to start talks to end war in Ukraine
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 6.30am ET.

London (UKX) -0.67% UK economy expands 0.1% in Q4 2024, estimates showed.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.54% DAX sets new all-time high; Germany's inflation eases to 2.3% in January.

France (CAC:IND) +1.35%

Romanian industrial output dropped 3.4% in December.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.73% on Thursday, maintaining a positive momentum from earlier this week, as traders assessed a host of earnings and economic data, and potential Ukraine-Russia peace talks.

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he had spoken to both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and that both leaders wanted peace.

On the corporate front, German technology conglomerate Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) was among top performers after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter profits.

Nestle's (OTCPK:NSRGY) full-year sales growth declined in line with expectations, while Commerzbank (OTCPK:CRZBF) said it would cut 3,900 jobs as it unveils new targets.

Coming up in the session: Eurozone industrial production data awaited shortly,

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.60%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points at 2.45%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.53%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Ocwen Financial GAAP EPS of $4.13 misses by $5.74, revenue of $976M misses by $24M
data.description:
• Ocwen Financial press release (NYSE:ONIT): FY GAAP EPS of $4.13 misses by $5.74.
  • Revenue of $976M (-8.8% Y/Y) misses by $24M.
  • $86 billion in total servicing additions ($47 billion in subservicing additions).
  • Book value per share improved $4 year-over-year to $56 as of December 31, 2024.
  • Reduced corporate debt by $145 million; debt-to-equity ratio of 2.96 to 1.

  • 2025 Outlook:

    • Increased adjusted ROE* guidance to 16% - 18%

data.title: Molson Coors rallies after Q4 results were boosted by pricing and sales mix
data.description:
Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) sailed past consensus estimates with its Q4 earnings report and set a favorable outlook.

Financial volume decreased 6.4% during the quarter, primarily due to lower shipments in both segments, including lower contract brewing volumes in the Americas. Brand volumes decreased 3.4%, including a 6.9% decrease in EMEA&APAC as well as a 2.2% decrease in the Americas.

Net sales decreased 2.0%, driven by lower financial volumes and unfavorable foreign currency impacts, partially offset by favorable price and sales mix. Net sales decreased 1.9% in constant currency.

Cost of goods sold ("COGS"): decreased 3.4% on a reported basis, primarily due to lower financial volumes, partially offset by higher cost of goods sold per hectoliter. EPS was reported at $1.30 vs. $1.13 consensus and $1.19 a year ago.

"We continued to advance our strategy and achieved another year of bottom-line growth. Amid a challenging macroeconomic environment, we continued to support the health of our brands globally and premiumize our business in several markets while developing plans for premiumization in the U.S. in 2025," noted CEO Gavin Hattersley.

In Canada, Coors Light remained the number one light beer in the industry and again grew segment market share amid a challenging industry backdrop. In EMEA&APAC, Ožjusko, the market leader in Croatia, along with the successful launch of legacy brand Caraiman in Romania helped to offset some impact of the increasingly competitive environment for Carling in the U.K.

Looking ahead, Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) expects 2025 sales to rise at a low single-digit rate on a constant currency basis. Underlying EPS is seen growing at a high single-digit rate.

Shares of Molson Coors (TAP) rose 5.14% premarket to $56.20 vs. the 52-week trading range of $49.19 to $69.18.
